reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 1 (Dead Code): PASS - validateDataColumn is called from gossip subscriber for all P2P data column messages. Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): PASS - The vulnerability is on the P2P gossip path, not Engine API. While local validators are semi-trusted, the bug allows BYPASSING all gossip validation regardless of whether the local validator is compromised or not. The vulnerability is in the gossip handler itself (L37-38 accepts self-peer messages unconditionally). Gate 3 (Code Verification): PASS - Code reading is factually correct. At validate_data_column.go:37-38, the function checks `if pid == s.cfg.p2p.PeerID()` and immediately returns ValidationAccept, skipping all 12 REJECT checks (ValidFields, CorrectSubnet, ValidProposerSignature, SidecarInclusionProven, KZG verification, etc.). Gate 4 (Exploitability): PASS - An attacker can trigger this via a compromised local validator. The local validator is SEMI_TRUSTED per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json, and attacks requiring semi-trusted compromise are in-scope but may be lower severity. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e entry at outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:715-730 states 'After gossip validation acceptance, sidecar must have passed all REJECT checks in order.' The self-peer bypass violates this post-condition. Gate 6 (Scope Check): PASS - Fusaka-specific (PeerDAS data columns), in-scope. Severity: Original CRITICAL downgraded to HIGH. Client diversity caps severity: Prysm has 31% network share per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json. A single-client bug affecting 31% of validators cannot cause >50% network impact (CRITICAL threshold), but exceeds 33% for slashing or network splits, meeting HIGH criteria. Attack requires compromised local validator (SEMI_TRUSTED), which is a conditional but in-scope vector.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: After gossip validation acceptance, sidecar must have passed all REJECT checks in order. (PROP-6a4369e9-post-002, outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:715-730)
